LanguageData.USAC_AssaultRig.label: Orbital assault rig
LanguageData.USAC_AssaultRig.helpText:
Armed groups produce and deploy these flexibly deployable drilling rigs to the surface via on-orbit industrial platforms to carry out mining operations. Their mining methods cause severe surface pollution, which you can prevent by destroying them to contain the pollution and avoid deposit loss.
Alternatively, you can allow them to leave on their own after mining is complete. Regardless, armed groups will deploy troops and supplies to protect their property from intrusion.
LanguageData.USAC_Bodybag.label: Bodybag
LanguageData.USAC_Bodybag.helpText:
Bodybag is a designated packaging container for medical departments to package organic materials in transactions. USAC have a long history of legally acquiring such unclaimed materials for peaceful research, biological experiments, and medical aesthetics.
Bodybag containing samples must be placed within trade beacon range. Furthermore, materials with drug resistance can provide additional market value.
It is important to note that body bags cannot prevent environmental microorganisms from damaging the integrity of the materials; therefore, for large-scale long-term preservation, large cold storage facilities should be considered to prevent the loss of value due to natural decay.
LanguageData.USAC_CreditBonds.label: Credit bonds
LanguageData.USAC_CreditBonds.helpText: Armed groups and related traders used a form of value called credit bonds as a substitute for silver in the trade. These bonds could only be obtained through specific material transactions, and their registered nature made them difficult to circulate through other means.
